questions.prompt: Which of the following is a major drawback of traditional file processing systems that motivates the use of a DBMS?

options.text: Data redundancy and inconsistency across files

questions.explanation: File processing systems duplicate the same data across multiple files maintained by different applications, leading to redundancy and inconsistencies. A DBMS centralizes data and enforces integrity to remove this issue.

questions.prompt: In the three-schema (ANSI/SPARC) architecture of a DBMS, the level that describes how the data is actually stored on disk is the:
questions.explanation: The internal (physical) level describes the actual storage layout — data files, indices, hash tables, etc. The conceptual level describes the logical structure, and external level describes user-specific views.

questions.prompt: The ability to change the conceptual schema of a database without affecting application programs that use it is known as:
questions.explanation: Logical data independence means changes to the logical (conceptual) schema, such as adding a new attribute, do not require rewriting external applications. Physical data independence concerns changes only to the storage layer.

questions.prompt: In an Extended Entity-Relationship (EER) diagram, which symbol/concept is used to represent that one entity set is a specialization of another?

options.text: A triangle labelled 'ISA' (or a circle/triangle with a sub-class line)

questions.explanation: Specialization/generalization in EER is represented using an 'ISA' triangle (or circle with disjoint/overlapping markers) connecting the superclass to its subclasses. Diamonds represent relationships, double ellipses represent multivalued attributes.

questions.prompt: A weak entity set in an ER diagram is best characterized by which of the following?

options.text: It has its own primary key independent of any other entity set
Its existence depends on a strong (owner) entity set, and it is identified using a partial key combined with the owner's key
It can never participate in any relationship
It must always be a multi-valued attribute

questions.explanation: A weak entity does not have a sufficient key of its own and is identified by combining its partial (discriminator) key with the primary key of the owner entity through an identifying relationship (drawn with double diamonds/rectangles).
